summary: Godot Editor freezes after a few minutes of use (no errors in console)

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.2-4.4.1

I haven't tested earlier versions like 4.1 or 4.0, but I can do that if needed.

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3050 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7283) - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-12650H (16 threads)

### Issue description

After a few minutes of using the Godot editor, it completely freezes and becomes unresponsive. This happens regardless of whether I'm using the .NET version or the standard version of Godot.

I tried launching from command line to catch possible logs, but the freeze happens silently. No crash logs or errors appear in the terminal or output window, even when using debug flags like --gpu-abort.

Happens in all existing project, usually after 5 hours since creating.

### Steps to reproduce

If project exists:
1. Open any project.
2. Work in the editor for a few minutes 
3. Editor suddenly freezes and must be force-closed.

If project is new:
1. Create new project
2. Work in the editor for a some hours
3. Save, close and open this one
4. Editor suddenly freezes after some minutes (even seconds).

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Can be reproduced with an empty project (as I marked earlier)

summary: Fix incorrect transform when editor camera is moved externally

description.text:
<!--
Please target the `master` branch in priority.

Relevant fixes are cherry-picked for stable branches as needed by maintainers.

To speed up the contribution process and avoid CI errors, please set up pre-commit hooks locally:
https://docs.godotengine.org/en/latest/contributing/development/code_style_guidelines.html
-->

Related: https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/11424#issuecomment-2817938811

When modifying the global transform directly the result ends up being wrong. The method `_apply_camera_transform_to_cursor()` didn't fully deconstruct the externally modified transform for the cursor data that would be used to transform the camera when the update for it is called. 

It is not applying the cursor / z distance to the position of the cursor.

Note how the camera is translated here:

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/2d3bdcac35ac22ee6c4d5d5edc88ba947d0659d5/editor/plugins/node_3d_editor_plugin.cpp#L627-L640

Additionally, interpolation is removed when modified externally because it is unnecessary (or can be done externally) and leads to minor inaccuracies. 

Before:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bd676a9e-5884-4e6b-b04e-2e992c7d0c5d

After:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f8c02e8e-f537-4bd7-bea6-f4b98331eabd


How to test:

Download Zylann `Editor Debugger` addon and enable it:

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cfcb8be2-b8c8-405f-91c4-7d376433977d)

Check `Show in Inspector`: 

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3ed7cb43-244f-46ef-b0ed-8f69c22c72dc)

Use the addon's `F12` functionality to select the 3D viewport (this will select a `Control` node which is on top), then navigate from there to the `Camera3D` node.

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8952160c-fb65-42dd-81d3-0866a1a919da)

Open the inspector and manipulate the Camera's transform.

summary: EditorPlugin: handles(object) and edit(object) not called for custom node type despite plugin activation

description.text:
### Tested versions

v4.4.1.stable.steam

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable (49a5bc7b6) - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Mobile) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7270) - AMD Ryzen 5 7600X 6-Core Processor (12 threads)

### Issue description

An `EditorPlugin` designed to handle a custom `Node3D` type (defined with `@tool` and `class_name`) fails to have its `handles(object)` and `edit(object)` methods invoked when an instance of the custom node is selected in the editor's Scene tree. This prevents the plugin from recognizing the node it's supposed to edit and blocks core plugin functionality like displaying contextual UI or handling specific input events for that node type.

### Steps to reproduce

1.  **Create Custom Node Script (`hex_tilemap_3d.gd`):**
    ```gdscript
    # hex_tilemap_3d.gd
    @tool
    class_name HexTileMap3D
    extends Node3D
    func _ready():
        pass
    ```

2.  **Create Plugin Configuration (`addons/hex_editor/plugin.cfg`):**
    ```ini
    [plugin]
    name="HexTileMap3D Editor Test"
    description="Test plugin for HexTileMap3D node handling."
    author="Test User"
    version="0.1"
    script="hex_editor_plugin.gd"
    ```

3.  **Create Plugin Script (`addons/hex_editor/hex_editor_plugin.gd`):**
    ```gdscript
    # addons/hex_editor/hex_editor_plugin.gd
    @tool
    extends EditorPlugin

    const HexTileMap3DClass = preload("res://hex_tilemap_3d.gd")
    var current_hexmap_instance = null

    func _get_plugin_name() -> String:
        return "HexTileMap3D Editor Test"

    func _enter_tree():
        print("Hex Editor Plugin: _enter_tree")
        # Optional: Add set_input_event_forwarding_always_enabled() here for further testing

    func _exit_tree():
        print("Hex Editor Plugin: _exit_tree")

    func handles(object) -> bool:
        print("Hex Editor Plugin: handles() - START - Object: ", object) # Debug Print
        var object_script = object.get_script() if is_instance_valid(object) and object.has_method("get_script") else null
        var handled = object_script == HexTileMap3DClass
        print("Hex Editor Plugin: handles() - Script: ", object_script, " | Handled: ", handled) # Debug Print
        return handled

    func edit(object):
        print("Hex Editor Plugin: edit() - START - Object: ", object) # Debug Print
        var object_script = object.get_script() if is_instance_valid(object) and object.has_method("get_script") else null
        if object_script == HexTileMap3DClass:
            current_hexmap_instance = object
            print("Hex Editor Plugin: edit() - Assigned hexmap instance.") # Debug Print
        else:
            if current_hexmap_instance == object:
                current_hexmap_instance = null
                print("Hex Editor Plugin: edit() - Cleared hexmap instance.") # Debug Print
            else:
                 print("Hex Editor Plugin: edit() - Different node selected.") # Debug Print


    func make_visible(visible):
        print("Hex Editor Plugin: make_visible() - Visible: ", visible) # Debug Print

    func _forward_3d_gui_input(camera: Camera3D, event: InputEvent) -> int:
        print("Hex Editor Plugin: _forward_3d_gui_input() - Event: ", event) # Debug Print
        if not is_instance_valid(current_hexmap_instance):
             print("Hex Editor Plugin: _forward_3d_gui_input() - No valid hexmap instance.") # Debug Print
             return EditorPlugin.AFTER_GUI_INPUT_PASS

        if event is InputEventMouseButton and event.pressed:
             print("Hex Editor Plugin: _forward_3d_gui_input() - Mouse button pressed!") # Debug Print
             return EditorPlugin.AFTER_GUI_INPUT_STOP # Consume click

        return EditorPlugin.AFTER_GUI_INPUT_PASS
    ```

4.  **Setup Scene:** Create a new 3D scene. Add a `Node3D`. Attach the `hex_tilemap_3d.gd` script to it. Save the scene.
5.  **Activate Plugin:** Go to `Project` -> `Project Settings` -> `Plugins` and set the "HexTileMap3D Editor Test" plugin to **Active**.
6.  **Restart Editor:** Close and reopen the Godot editor.
7.  **Observe Output:** Check the Output panel. The `_enter_tree` message should appear.
8.  **Select Node:** Click on the `Node3D` (with the `hex_tilemap_3d.gd` script) in the Scene tree.
9.  **Click Viewport:** Click in the 3D viewport.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[test-project.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19830106/test-project.zip)

summary: Improve Path2D debug performance

description.text:
Improves `Path2D` node debug performance by changing the debug to use mesh and multimesh instead of the slower canvas line draw functions.

This PR removes the old remnants of what was a fake open-gl line anti-aliasing that no longer works (it rendered everything twice with 0.5 alpha, aka very efficient).

The PR also removes the debug path width that did not really work and caused a lot of rendering issues on high res and dpi displays or different zoom and scale levels in the editor. It is now all thin "hardware" mesh lines.

![linezoom](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ad912757-cd03-4f1f-a19d-8e224227be85)

This largely means it replaces the old debug rendering. For editing paths the internal arrays got replaced with more efficient LocalVectors that keep the memory between path edits.

The rather slow canvas item draw for each segment and point were replaced with a combination of using static meshes for path lines and several multimeshes for the rendering of the editor handles and textures.

The nodes now always render the static debug visuals while the editor plugin only renders the editing parts like handles of the selected node on top.

![thick](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/11a065c4-10f3-4ee2-b2a5-6a907828cb30)

The result is a very noticeable performance boost editing and rendering many complex 2d paths at the same time.

summary: SpinBox unexpected behaviour for min/max values equal to -INF/INF or big values

description.text:
### Tested versions

 - Reproducible in v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Windows 10 - Vulkan (Forward+) - RTX 3060 laptop - AMD Ryzen 5 5600H - 24GB RAM

### Issue description

Creating a SpinBox whose _min/max_ values are -INF / INF will make it always show "nan" even if the value is changed at running time.

Similarly, if _min/max_ values are defined as big values like -1e100 / 1e100, then it will always show "0.0" even if the value is changed at running time.

It's interesting that this unexpected behavior only happens if `step > 0`, if _step_ is _0.0_ then the SpinBox will work as expected showing the value correctly.

Note from Godot Docs: "The maximum value of float is approximately 1.79769e308, and the minimum is approximately -1.79769e308." I believe SpinBox should support this range of values.

Note 2: Maybe this behavior is present in all _range_ nodes and not only in SpinBox.

### Steps to reproduce

Create a SpinBox node and add the following code:

```
extends SpinBox

func _ready() -> void:
	min_value = -1e100
	max_value = 1e100
	step = 0.001
	value = 1234.123
```
Unexpectedly, the SpinBox always shows "0.0", even if the value is changed

You can also try the following code:

```
extends SpinBox

func _ready() -> void:
	min_value = -INF
	max_value = INF
	step = 0.001
	value = 1234.123
```
Unexpectedly, the SpinBox always shows "nan", even if the value is changed

(Setting step to 0.0 will make the SpinBox work as expected, if `step > 0`, then the _bug_ persists)

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[SpinBoxNAN.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19826825/SpinBoxNAN.zip)

summary: D3D12 OpenXR backend may not create depth buffers properly.

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible with PR 104207 (unmerged).
- Not applicable to any prior version.

### System information

Windows 10 - PR 104207 (unmerged) - Forward renderer (godot-xr-template) - Intel ARC 310 (driver 101.6734)

### Issue description

I raised this issue in https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/104207 during final testing of the new code. This is not applicable yet to any public version of Godot (though is slated for Godot 4.5).

When running the godot-xr-template with D3D12 rendering on an Intel ARC GPU with validation layers enabled, the following error is raised:

> D3D12 ERROR: ID3D12CommandList::OMSetRenderTargets: The RenderTargetView at slot 0 is not compatible with the DepthStencilView. When D3D12_FEATURE_DATA_D3D12_OPTIONS19::MismatchingOutputDimensionsSupported is FALSE, DepthStencilViews may only be used with RenderTargetViews if the effective dimensions of the Views are equal, as well as the Resource types, multisample count, and multisample quality. One exception is that DepthStencil width and/or height can be larger than RenderTarget width/height.The RenderTargetView at slot 0 has (w:2496,h:2592,as:2), while the Resource is a Texture2D with (mc:1,mq:0). The DepthStencilView has (w:2496,h:2592,as:1), while the Resource is a Texture2D with (mc:1,mq:0).  [ EXECUTION ERROR #728: SET_RENDER_TARGETS_INVALID]

While the outcome is still positive (rendering looks OK), this could be sign of a deeper issues that may manifest itself with a larger project.

It appears that maybe the engine is not allocating the depth buffer with the correct array size, in which case there could be depth issues with stereo rendering.

summary: Incorrect rendering of DisplayServer.window_set_size and RenderingServer.viewport_set_size

description.text:
### Tested versions

v4.6.dev1.official [8d8041bd4], v4.5.stable.official [876b29033], v4.5.dev2.official [af2c713]

### System information

Godot v4.5.dev2

### Issue description

I encountered an issue where, when using DisplayServer.window_set_size(...), the window expands or shrinks, but the render does not follow it, although the render itself continues working normally. Additionally, I would note that in the video, you can see the same issue #105419, where the window appears in one of the corners of the screen and then only gets centered.

summary: Add editor option to toggle idle parsing

description.text:
While idle parsing is a useful feature, it can be distracting sometimes.

---

This PR adds an editor option to toggle idle parsing altogether. When disabled, the script editor will no longer perform idle parsing. The script will then only validate if it is reopened, or manually saved (via keybinding).

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/072a7c41-a343-4d8a-90f8-9fe57814a829)

~~Additionally, this PR fixes the fact that saving a script doesn't trigger validation. This is most noticeable with long idle parsing delays, and has briefly been mentioned in the [Godot proposals](https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/11081#issuecomment-2453511934) as a potential candidate for a PR.~~

~~**TODO:** Make saving files via the "File" drop-down menu also trigger validation. While it's probably not a frequent use case (I reckon most people save with `Ctrl+S`?), I'm open for ideas as I'm not sure how to implement it myself.~~

**EDIT**: The file saving bug fix will be submitted via a separate PR, as discussed below.

summary: [ScrollContainer] Add option to hold child at maximum scroll value when resized

description.text:
Adds `Resize Behavior` properties `hold_when_max_horizontal` and `hold_when_max_vertical` that keep the end of the `ScrollContainer` child pinned to the end of the `ScrollContainer` when resized. This is essential for things like a typical stack of messages that fill in from the `Control` bottom, or left side `SplitContainer` panels where it's unnatural for the current default behavior where the control is subsumed on the right side instead of shifting left as expected.

This PR essentially makes a top or left resizable `ScrollContainer` behave like a mirror image of a right or bottom resizeable `ScrollContainer`.

While this can be simulated by scripting using the `item_rect_changed` signal on the child `Control`, it is impossible to avoid nasty jittering of the child during resize, especially if the child `Control`changes size in the axis of the scroll after being added to the scene tree.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/49eabf93-353b-4465-9b6c-aef9268e74d5

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/21cd8da5-7ffa-460a-ab6c-01d9394171f4

summary: Rendering of my game very often randomly freezes after startup if I use fullscreen exclusive mode.

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.4.1 (both gdscript and c# versions)

### System information

Windows 11 - intel i5 13600kf - rtx 4070 (driver 32.0.15.6636) and mobile rtx 2060 (different driver version)

### Issue description

**EDIT: I've removed all scripts from my scene and only kept a shader with a new material which did not exist when I was making this report (to make sure it's a new addition to avoid this being a previous issue), this shader just seamlessly moves a noise texture on the screen to have something moving, no other scripts are now attached at all (!), the test scene now has a Node2D as a parent with a Camera2D which has a ColorRect attached with the uv wrapping shader metnioned above. Despite all this he game still keeps freezing in the exclusive fullscreen (not windowed) on start up, so this is definitely not my code issue.
I've also started another project with the above and still the same problem.
Here's a video how it looks, it happens all the time:**

https://youtu.be/zMK7q1N0XIQ

(this issue happens only with `exclusive fullscreen` mode, not `fullscreen` or `windowed` modes.)

---

My 2D game works perfectly fine if it's in the windowed mode but if I set the window mode in Project Settings to Fullscreen Exclusive my game randomly stops rendering and never recovers after the startup (it renderes a single frame, from what I can say visually, which is about a fraction of a second - somewhere in a range of 0.5 second - into the game play and then the screen gets stuck at that one frame - it never shows the frames from that first 0.5s which led to the freeze).

The rest of the game from what I can say seems to be working:
 - Audio works, I can hear the background music, I can hear new enemies spawning, I can hear battles and I can eventually hear the palyer dying and losing the game.
 - I added print to a _Process and _Input methods to see if it prints anything and to check if the tree is paused but the printing also works fine and the tree is not paused the only thing is that the prints from both input and process methods are slow like they happen just once a half second or so and sometimes it just prints multiple lines out straight and such it's like the whole engine things it's not in focus or something?

No errors, no warnings in the editor or the console.

It happens when I play the game in the editor but also in the exported (release) project.

It happens about once in about 2-3 start ups or so, meaning it's very common.

summary: get_active_material() throws an error on MeshInstance3D that has not yet had a mesh set on it yet when running from a @tool script

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot 4.5.dev2

### System information

Win 10

### Issue description

get_active_material() will throw an error instead of null if it is directed at a MeshInstance3D that has not had a mesh added to it yet.

But it will only throw the error if it is being run from a tool script. if a script runs on the MeshInstance3D then it will return null as normal.

The error it throws is:
`ERROR: scene/3d/mesh_instance_3d.cpp:372 - Index p_surface = 0 is out of bounds (surface_override_materials.size() = 0).`


### Steps to reproduce

Load up the supplied minimal reproduction project

Run the project, Note the output terminal showing <Object#null> as it should.

Now go to the scripts and right click on new_script.gd and select run. Note the error:

`ERROR: scene/3d/mesh_instance_3d.cpp:372 - Index p_surface = 0 is out of bounds (surface_override_materials.size() = 0).`

Now click on the MeshInstance3D node and add a BoxMesh and then immediately clear it.

Now go to the scripts and right click on new_script.gd and select run. Note the error is gone.

Save scene and close Godot and re-open the project go scripts and run new_script.gd again and note that same error has returned.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[get_active_material.test.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19824257/get_active_material.test.zip)

_**EditorInspector**_ tooltips seems to be affected by waccom drivers after https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/95044

Disabling the internal process of the tooltip's EditorHelpBitTooltip solves this issue.
Everything is still working fine on windows. But some testing in other OS's would be appreciated.

Fixed:


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6ade3a4a-0a2e-44b7-8cab-d049f0a922b7


PS: Feel free to close this PR if someone more knowledgeable knows how to fix this issue in a better way.

summary: Overriding _GetRid() on Material does nothing
[Web] Cache feature support checks to reduce JS interop overhead

description.text:
This PR adds a cache to `_check_internal_feature_support()` to avoid redundant JS calls.

While profiling my game, I noticed that each key input triggered ~228 calls to `godot_js_os_has_feature`, introducing significant overhead due to UTF-8 string conversions and JS interop.

This change reduces that to a single call per unique feature, improving performance with no behavior changes.

summary: Godot incorrectly uses 32-bit .NET SDK even when 64-bit is installed

description.text:
### Tested versions

can be reproduced at v4.2.2.stable.mono.official [15073afe3]

### System information

Godot v4.2.2.stable.mono - Windows 10.0.22000 - Vulkan (Mobile) - integrated Intel(R) Iris(R) Xe Graphics (Intel Corporation; 32.0.101.6078) - 11th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-1165G7 @ 2.80GHz (8 Threads)

### Issue description

# Description
When both 32-bit and 64-bit .NET SDKs are installed on Windows, Godot detects the correct 64-bit SDK version (e.g., 8.0.15) but still attempts to use the 32-bit SDK during compilation. When opening any normal C# Godot project, console said:
```
/root/godot/modules/mono/editor/GodotTools/GodotTools/GodotSharpEditor.cs:471 - .NET Sdk not found. The required version is '8.0.15'.
  /root/godot/modules/mono/editor/GodotTools/GodotTools/GodotSharpEditor.cs:416 - System.IO.FileNotFoundException: Could not load file or assembly 'Microsoft.Build, Version=15.1.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b03f5f7f11d50a3a'. 系统找不到指定的文件。
  File name: 'Microsoft.Build, Version=15.1.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b03f5f7f11d50a3a'
     at GodotTools.ProjectEditor.ProjectUtils.Open(String path)
     at GodotTools.GodotSharpEditor.ApplyNecessaryChangesToSolution() in /root/godot/modules/mono/editor/GodotTools/GodotTools/GodotSharpEditor.cs:line 416
--- Debug adapter server started ---
--- GDScript language server started on port 6005 ---
```
(Chinese inside means "System cannot find the specified file.")
# Expected Behavior
Godot should prioritize the 64-bit .NET SDK when both architectures are present, especially if the OS is 64-bit.
# Actual Behavior
Godot incorrectly selects the 32-bit SDK, leading to build failures.
# Environment
- Godot Version: v4.2.2.stable.mono.official [15073afe3]
- OS: Windows 11 x64
- .NET SDKs or runtimes:
8.0.15 (x64) at `C:\Program Files\dotnet`
8.0.14 (x86) at `C:\Program Files (x86)\dotnet` (only windowsdesktop-runtime)
```
Godot v4.2.2.stable.mono - Windows 10.0.22000 - Vulkan (Mobile) - integrated Intel(R) Iris(R) Xe Graphics (Intel Corporation; 32.0.101.6078) - 11th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-1165G7 @ 2.80GHz (8 Threads)
```
# Additional Context
This issue does not occur if only the 64-bit SDK is installed.
Workaround: Manually uninstall the 32-bit SDK forces Godot to use the 64-bit version.


### Steps to reproduce

# Reproduce
1. Install both 32-bit and 64-bit .NET SDK (or runtimes):
8.0.15 (x64) at `C:\Program Files\dotnet`
8.0.14 (x86) at `C:\Program Files (x86)\dotnet` ( this version: `https://builds.dotnet.microsoft.com/dotnet/WindowsDesktop/8.0.14/windowsdesktop-runtime-8.0.14-win-x86.exe` )
2. Open any C# project with Godot (`v4.2.2.stable.mono.official [15073afe3]`).
3. Observe the error logs in Godot output panel

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Use this to get a error-able project.
```
git clone https://gitee.com/acrsinx/1024.git
```

summary: Godot LSP doesn't handle replies without methods (e.g. workspace/applyEdit after connecting a signal)

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible on Godot 4.4.1
- Reproducible on master/[2d3bdcac35ac22ee6c4d5d5edc88ba947d0659d5]

### System information

Godot v4.5.dev (2d3bdcac3) - EndeavourOS #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Thu, 10 Apr 2025 18:43:59 +0000 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3090 (nvidia; 570.133.07) - AMD Ryzen 9 7900X 12-Core Processor (24 threads)

### Issue description

Godot's LSP doesn't know how to handle replies from the client (these replies have no `method` field,  only an `id`).

The only instance I have notice this happening is after connecting a signal to a node from the editor, which adds a boilerplate handler.

For instance, when adding a signal to a script from the editor, the Godot LSP sends a `workspace/applyEdit` to the LSP client:

```
"rpc.receive"	{ id = 7, jsonrpc = "2.0", method = "workspace/applyEdit", params = { edit = { changes = { ["file:///home/charles/Games/Testing/godot-lsp/test.gd"] = { { newText = "\n\nfunc _on_editor_description_changed(node:Node) -> void:\n\tpass # Replace with function body.\n", range = { ["end"] = { character = 0, line = 22 }, start = { character = 0, line = 22 } } } } } } } }
```

When the client has applied the edit, it replies that it was succesful with a reply to the same ID (notice the `id = 7` in both message an response):

```
"rpc.send"	{ id = 7, jsonrpc = "2.0", result = { applied = true } }
```

Whn Godot recieves this message, it replies with and error:
```
"rpc.receive"	{ error = { code = -32601, message = "Method not found: " }, id = 7, jsonrpc = "2.0" }
"No callback found for server response id 7"
```

It appears Godot doesn't know how to handle client replies, specifically anything which doesn't have a `method`.

The `workspace/applyEdit` spec (https://microsoft.github.io/language-server-protocol/specifications/lsp/3.17/specification/#workspace_applyEdit) says the client should reply this this message when it's applied.

Happy to provide more information on request. I'm also reachable on Discord (same username).

### Steps to reproduce

I am using Neovim's LSP implementation, although I have not yet tested with other LSP clients (e.g. VS Code).

1. Configure an external editor. I am using Neovim (with AstroNvim with AstroCommunity's Godot pack which configures the LSP. I'm happy to provide information how to set this up)
1. Create a new scene, add a node, attach a script to the scene
1. Open the script file in the LSP client/editor (this should be done automatically when creating the script)
1. Connect a signal on the node to the script
1. This should throw the error within the client as the server (Godot) has replied with an error

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

summary: Game view: in Window canvas_items or viewport Stretch mode and Game view stretch to fit mode, 2D camera override middle button drag causes camera to drift away

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Ubuntu 22.04.5 LTS 22.04 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce GTX 860M (nvidia; 535.183.01) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-4710HQ CPU @ 2.50GHz (8 threads)

### Issue description

In my 2D game, I was testing the new Game view: Override Camera (2D selection mode). I tried to drag the view holding the mouse middle button, but whatever direction I choose, the camera moves much faster in the downward direction. Soon, I cannot see the scene anymore.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9f7c7e3b-195c-44ba-99b6-8958bb30c433

I create a MWE and found out that the minimal Project settings to repro are:
- Project Settings > Window > Stretch mode: canvas_items or viewport
- Game view Stretch mode: Stretch to fit (see screenshot below)
- Game view Camera override active in default mode "Manipulate In-Game"

and of course you need to enable 2D selection or your Input will be redirected to the game.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/34dafe29-036a-4494-93e1-a40f6d73e332)

At different aspect ratio, I also found that the camera could drift to the right (the viewport content moves to the left).

I haven't tested 3D, it seems that it works differently though (I saw other bug reports about other issues like scrolling to zoom).

While testing this I also found issues on the other stretch modes at low resolutions, each a different issue... I will post them gradually.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a scene with a Sprite2D with Godot icon (you don't need a Camera2D node)
2. Project Settings > Window > Stretch mode: canvas_items or viewport
3. Game view Stretch mode: Stretch to fit
3. Play the game in embedded game view
4. Select 2D selection mode
5. Toggle Camera Override (keep default "Manipulate In-Game")
6. Mouse middle button drag on the viewport in any direction

summary: Two minor issues with anisotropic filtering in the Vulkan renderers

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in Godot 4.0.4.stable and Godot 4.4.1.stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable unknown - Arch Linux #1 ZEN SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Thu, 10 Apr 2025 18:43:47 +0000 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated Intel(R) Iris(R) Xe Graphics (RPL-P) - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-13900H (20 threads)

### Issue description

In the Vulkan renderers (Forward+ and Mobile), on my laptop's Intel Iris Xe integrated graphics with Mesa as the GPU driver, there are 2 slight issues with anisotropic filtering which don't happen in the Compatibility renderer.

The first is that setting "Project Settings > Rendering > Textures > Anisotropic Filtering Level" to "Disabled" doesn't actually disable it. Instead, it looks the same as when it is set to 2x.

The second issue is that when a material's sampling mode is set to "Nearest Mipmap Anisotropic," it looks the same as "Linear Mipmap Anisotropic." On the other hand, in the OpenGL renderer, it looks the same as "Nearest Mipmap," which is a less unexpected result.

### Steps to reproduce

For the first issue:

1. Create a project in the Forward+ or Mobile renderer
2. Create a MeshInstance3D
3. Add a material, then set the sampling mode to "Linear Mipmap Anisotropic"
4. Go to "Project Settings > Rendering > Textures" and set Anisotropic Filtering Level to "Disabled"

For the second issue:

1. Create a project in the Forward+ or Mobile renderer
2. Create a MeshInstance3D
3. Add a material, then set the sampling mode to "Nearest Mipmap Anisotropic"

summary: LSP file rename/delete doesn't rename/delete .uid file

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in Godot 4.4.1

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable unknown - EndeavourOS #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Thu, 10 Apr 2025 18:43:59 +0000 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3090 (nvidia; 570.133.07) - AMD Ryzen 9 7900X 12-Core Processor (24 threads)

### Issue description

The behavior of renaming/deleting files in the FileSystem dock and with the LSP does not match. When renaming a file within the FileSytem dock, the matching `.uid` file is also renamed, but not from with LSP (it deletes the old one and creates a new one with a different UID). Same thing when deleting a file (it delete the `.uid`, but not instantly).


FileSystem dock rename: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/09ea7bc6a3058505dbb4138d435292b74668b2b9/editor/filesystem_dock.cpp#L1468

The operations that's missing from the LSP rename/delete:
- Move/delete `.uid`
- Move/delete `.import`
- Updates scene file path if they are opened in the editor (this one is less likely to happen, since usually code is expected to be edited with an external editor)

Currently, the LSP is missing implementations of https://microsoft.github.io/language-server-protocol/specifications/lsp/3.17/specification/#workspace_didRenameFiles, and has an incomplete implementation of https://microsoft.github.io/language-server-protocol/specifications/lsp/3.17/specification/#workspace_didDeleteFiles

### Steps to reproduce

- Create a `test.gd` and `icon.png` file
- Open an editor configured to use Godot's LSP. I'm using Neovim (with neo-tree/nvim-lsp-file-operations), but I'm pretty sure VS Code behaves the same
- Rename `test.gd`->`test2.gd` within the LSP editor, you should see `test.gd.uid` and `test2.gd.uid` (newly generated by Godot, with a new UID)
- Rename `icon.png`->`icon2.png` within the LSP editor, you should see `icon.png.import` and `icon2.png.import` (newly generated by Godot, with a new import settings)

summary: Add convert indent on save filter option in editor settings

description.text:
<!--
Please target the `master` branch in priority.

Relevant fixes are cherry-picked for stable branches as needed by maintainers.

To speed up the contribution process and avoid CI errors, please set up pre-commit hooks locally:
https://docs.godotengine.org/en/latest/contributing/development/code_style_guidelines.html
-->
Fixes #86092

### Summary

These changes add a "Convert Indent on Save Filter" option to the editor settings under Text Editor/Behavior/Files. This filter specifies which files have their indentation automatically converted on save (if the "Convert Indent on Save" option is enabled).

For example, putting `*.gd` under the filter option would mean any GDScript file would have their indentation automatically converted on save. Additionally, saving wouldn't modify the indentation of a file that doesn't match the pattern (like a JSON or text file).

### Backwards Compatibility

Some users could rely on the current behavior where all files have their indentation automatically converted. By default, the "Convert Indent on Save Filter" option is left empty and any file will be automatically converted on save. 

### Support for Multiple File Types

Since the user may want to automatically indent multiple file types, the "Convert Indent on Save Filter" option supports a comma-separated list. As an example, a user could specify `*.gd, *.cs, *.h, *.cpp` for the filter.

4. Put focus away from that window.
5. Put focus back into the window by clicking on the dark border (NOT the viewport itself).
6. Game doesn't receive input (other than mouse movement) and instead the editor itself receives it.
